question_text: An electromagnet is made by wrapping wire around an iron nail and connecting it to a battery. Explain why the electromagnet loses its magnetism when the battery is disconnected, but a permanent magnet does not.
correct_answer: When the battery is connected, electric current flows through the wire and creates a magnetic field that magnetises the iron nail (induced magnetism). When the battery is disconnected, the current stops, so the magnetic field disappears and the nail loses its magnetism. A permanent magnet has atoms already aligned in one direction, so it keeps its magnetism without needing electricity.
explanation: Electromagnets only work when electricity flows through them, but permanent magnets have their atoms already arranged to create magnetism, so they don't need electricity to keep their magnetic field.
